scenarios.description: No tokens from scan results in immediate endDocument (loop-0, branch:false)
scenarios.precondition: CssTokenIterator stub returns hasNext(FILTER_S_CMNT_CDO_CDC)=false; uses stub iterator to avoid actual I/O
scenarios.expectedResult: CssContentHandler.startDocument() then endDocument() called exactly once; no exceptions

scenarios.description: Single ATKEYWORD token triggers handleAtRule then endDocument (loop-1, branch-true, branch:ATKEYWORD)
scenarios.precondition: CssTokenIterator stub returns one ATKEYWORD token then hasNext false; use simple stub iterator
scenarios.expectedResult: handleAtRule called once with that token; contentHandler.startDocument(), contentHandler.endDocument() each once; no exceptions
pseudocode: stub iterator: hasNext->true, next->token.type=ATKEYWORD; then hasNext->false;
assert handleAtRule invoked once and no exception

scenarios.description: Single non-ATKEYWORD token triggers handleRuleSet then endDocument (loop-1, branch-true=false, branch:non-ATKEYWORD)
scenarios.precondition: CssTokenIterator stub returns one IDENT token then hasNext false; use stub iterator
scenarios.expectedResult: handleRuleSet called once; contentHandler.startDocument() and endDocument() called; no exceptions
pseudocode: stub iterator: hasNext->true, next->token.type!=ATKEYWORD; then hasNext->false;
assert handleRuleSet invoked once; endDocument called

scenarios.description: Two tokens (first ATKEYWORD, then non-ATKEYWORD) cover loop-2 mixed sequence
scenarios.precondition: Stub iterator yields ATKEYWORD then IDENT then no more; minimal stubbing
scenarios.expectedResult: handleAtRule then handleRuleSet invoked in order; start/endDocument once; no exceptions
pseudocode: assert handleAtRule then handleRuleSet called; endDocument

scenarios.description: Three tokens (ATKEYWORD, ATKEYWORD, IDENT) cover loop-3 to exceed 2 iterations
scenarios.precondition: Stub iterator yields two ATKEYWORD then one IDENT; light stub
scenarios.expectedResult: Two handleAtRule and one handleRuleSet calls; start/endDocument; no exceptions
pseudocode: stub iterator: ATKEYWORD, ATKEYWORD, IDENT, end;
assert two handleAtRule and one handleRuleSet calls

scenarios.description: IOException from scan() is propagated (exception path)
scenarios.expectedResult: IOException is thrown; no startDocument or endDocument call

scenarios.description: CssException from scan() is propagated (exception path)
scenarios.expectedResult: CssException is thrown; no document events

scenarios.description: PrematureEOFException in handleRuleSet breaks loop and ends document (exception branch)
scenarios.precondition: Stub iterator yields one IDENT then handleRuleSet stub throws PrematureEOFException; mocking necessary to simulate EOF
scenarios.expectedResult: startDocument() then endDocument(); no propagate; handleRuleSet exception swallowed
pseudocode: stub iterator: IDENT; stub handleRuleSet throws PrematureEOFException;

scenarios.precondition: Stub iterator yields one ATKEYWORD then handleAtRule stub throws CssGrammarException
scenarios.expectedResult: CssGrammarException is thrown; contentHandler.startDocument() but no endDocument()
pseudocode: stub iterator: ATKEYWORD; stub handleAtRule throws CssGrammarException;
throws CssGrammarException; assert startDocument() called once;

scenarios.precondition: Stub iterator yields one IDENT then handleRuleSet stub throws CssGrammarException
scenarios.expectedResult: CssGrammarException is thrown; startDocument() invoked; endDocument() not invoked
pseudocode: stub iterator: IDENT; stub handleRuleSet throws CssGrammarException;
